title: 3 Bedroom Houses for Rent in Collinwood, Cleveland
metaTitle: 3 Bedroom Houses for Rent in Collinwood, Cleveland
metaDescription: 3 bedroom houses for rent in Collinwood, Cleveland (44110) — typically $1,000–$1,300/mo, Section 8-friendly. Book a free showing today.
primaryKeyword: 3 bedroom houses for rent Collinwood cleveland
answerBox: Three-bedroom houses in Collinwood, on Cleveland's far northeast side in ZIP code 44110, typically rent from about $1,000 to $1,300 a month. Most are early-1900s homes with a basement and yard, and every home Rent Finder Cleveland works with in Collinwood accepts Section 8 Housing Choice Vouchers.

sections.h2: What does a 3-bedroom house rent for in Collinwood?
body: Three-bedroom houses in Collinwood — Cleveland's far-northeast neighborhood centered in **ZIP code 44110** — typically rent from about $1,000 to $1,300 a month. That's at or below Zumper's July 4, 2026 citywide 3-bedroom median of $1,350/month, reflecting the neighborhood's older, individually owned housing stock rather than newer large-scale construction.

RentCafe's July 2, 2026 report, which tracks larger professionally-managed apartment buildings, put the citywide 3-bedroom average considerably higher at $2,716/month. Collinwood's 3-bedroom rentals are almost entirely single-family homes and duplex units built in the early 1900s, which is the main reason prices sit well below that apartment-focused figure.

sections.h2: What to expect inside a 3-bedroom Collinwood rental
body: Collinwood's housing stock dates largely to the early 1900s streetcar era, so 3-bedroom homes typically include a full basement (usually unfinished storage/utility space), a front porch, and a modest yard. Streets running north from St. Clair Avenue and Ivanhoe Road toward the lakefront tend to have larger, older homes, while blocks nearer Waterloo Road mix residential and small commercial buildings — exact layout and condition vary enough that a showing is the best way to confirm room sizes before applying.

A 3-bedroom layout suits larger households, roommates sharing rent, or anyone needing more space than a 2-bedroom unit offers. If two bedrooms would fit your situation better, browse our [houses for rent in Cleveland](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) hub, which lets you filter by bedroom count and budget citywide.

Because a 3-bedroom home has more space to heat than a smaller unit, it's worth asking about the furnace's age and whether storm windows are in place before signing — an older, drafty 3-bedroom house can run a noticeably higher winter heating bill than a well-maintained one listed at the same rent.

rows: Most requested size for larger households

sections.h2: Getting around from a 3-bedroom home in Collinwood
body: Collinwood is served by multiple GCRTA bus routes connecting to downtown Cleveland, and the Red Line's Windermere station — the heavy-rail line's eastern terminus, connecting to Cleveland Hopkins Airport and downtown — sits at the edge of the neighborhood. Drivers can reach I-90 (Lakeland Freeway) within a few minutes from most Collinwood streets, useful for larger households juggling more than one commute. The Waterloo Arts District along Waterloo Road adds nearby shops and dining, and the North Shore Collinwood section along Lake Erie offers additional green space.

For a larger household with more than one vehicle or more frequent errands, having both a rail option at Windermere and quick highway access via I-90 can matter more than it would for a smaller household with a single commute to manage.

sections.h2: Section 8 and larger households in Collinwood
body: Every 3-bedroom home Rent Finder Cleveland works with in Collinwood accepts Section 8 / Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready — notable because larger vouchers (3- and 4-bedroom) can be harder to place than smaller ones. Vouchers themselves are issued by CMHA, which accepts preliminary applications year-round through its [online portal](https://www.cmha.net/housing/applicants/housing_choice_vouchers.php); selection runs through periodic lottery draws rather than strict order of application. Ohio has no statewide source-of-income law and Cleveland has no local ordinance requiring voucher acceptance, so always confirm directly with any owner outside our managed homes.

sections.h2: Applying for a 3-bedroom home in Collinwood
body: Larger homes tend to draw more interest relative to available supply, since 3-bedroom units make up a smaller share of Collinwood's rental stock than 2-bedroom units. Every applicant is screened the same way regardless of bedroom count, but we do welcome Housing Choice Vouchers and will consider references or a co-signer if your credit history is limited.

Given the added demand for larger units, the most reliable approach is to view a specific 3-bedroom Collinwood home as soon as it's listed and submit a complete application — proof of income, references, and voucher paperwork if applicable — right away.

sections.h2: Comparing 3-bedroom options across Cleveland's east side
body: If Collinwood's current availability doesn't have the 3-bedroom home you need, nearby neighborhoods we also work with homes in include [Slavic Village](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-slavic-village-cleveland/) and [Buckeye](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/3-bedroom-houses-for-rent-buckeye-cleveland/), which carries a similar 3-bedroom price range. [Book a showing](/p/book-showing) to see a specific home in person.

faq.q: How much does a 3-bedroom house rent for in Collinwood?
faq.a: Three-bedroom houses in Collinwood typically rent from about $1,000 to $1,300 a month, at or below Cleveland's citywide 3-bedroom median of $1,350 reported by Zumper in July 2026.

faq.q: What does a 3-bedroom Collinwood rental usually include?
faq.a: Most 3-bedroom homes in Collinwood are early-1900s single-family houses or duplex units with a full basement, a modest yard, and street or driveway parking. Layouts vary by house, so a showing is the best way to confirm room sizes.

Yes. Every 3-bedroom home Rent Finder Cleveland works with in Collinwood accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready. Vouchers are issued by CMHA; confirm acceptance directly with any landlord outside our managed homes.

faq.q: What ZIP code is Collinwood in?
faq.a: Collinwood is primarily in ZIP code 44110 on Cleveland's far northeast side, spanning the North Shore Collinwood lakefront area and the inland Collinwood-Nottingham section.

faq.q: How do I get downtown from a 3-bedroom home in Collinwood?
faq.a: Multiple GCRTA bus routes connect Collinwood to downtown Cleveland, and the Red Line's Windermere station sits at the neighborhood's edge. Drivers can reach I-90 within a few minutes for a direct route downtown or east.
